question_type: ["表格填空题", "单选题", "图片填空题", "句子填空题"]

content.cn_text: 我今天的演讲是关于工作动机的。
content.en_text: My lecture today is on motivation at work.

content.cn_text: 如果管理者想要改善组织的运作，了解员工的动力是什么对他们来说很重要——知道如何既满足抱负远大的员工，也满足那些没有抱负或能力的员工。
content.en_text: If managers are to improve the running of their organization, it's important for them to understand what motivates the workforce -to know how to satisfy both the high flyers and those who have lower ambitions or ability.

content.cn_text: 首先，让我们看看动机是如何起作用的模型。
content.en_text: First of all, let's look at the model of how motivation works.

content.cn_text: 你会发现，每个员工都有需求或期望，而这些需求和期望会影响每个人的行为——例如，如果他们知道自己会得到奖励，也就是说，如果他们能实现加薪或升职的目标，他们就会更加努力地工作。
content.en_text: You will see that every member of the workforce has needs or expectations , and these needs and expectations will affect each person's behaviour- For example, they will work harder if they know that they will be rewarded – that is, if they can achieve their goals of higher pay or promotion.

content.cn_text: 这些目标的实现反过来又会带来工作满意度。
content.en_text: The achievement of these goals will, in turn, lead to job satisfaction .

content.cn_text: 这就会反馈到员工的需求或期望中。
content.en_text: This then feeds back into the worker's needs or expectations.

content.cn_text: 多年来，不同的管理理论和不同的组织方法的发展影响了我们看待动机的方式。
content.en_text: Over the years the development of different theories of management and different approaches to organization has affected the way we view motivation.

content.cn_text: 19世纪，作家弗雷德里克·泰勒(Frederick Taylor)提出了工人只对赚钱感兴趣的理论。
content.en_text: In the 19th century, the writer Frederick Taylor put forward the theory that workers were only interested in making  money.

content.cn_text: 相比之下，在20世纪初，研究人员发现，人们去工作是为了满足一系列不同的需求，而不仅仅是为了经济回报。
content.en_text: In contrast, in the early twentieth century, researchers found that people went to work to satisfya range of different needs, and not simply for financial reward.

content.cn_text: 特别强调了一种需要，即社会需要。
content.en_text: One need, in particular, was emphasized the social need.

content.cn_text: 例如，一项针对一家煤矿公司的早期研究表明，如果人们能够在团队中工作，他们通常会更快乐，工作效率也会更高。
content.en_text: An early study done on a coal mining company, for instance, showed that people generally were happier and worked more productively if they were able to work in teams.

content.cn_text: 马斯洛在1943年提出了20世纪一个重要的动机理论。
content.en_text: One important twentieth-century theoiy of motivation was put forward by Maslow in 1943 .

content.cn_text: 马斯洛确定了五种重要的需求，他把它们放在一个三角形中:在三角形的底部，他把他所谓的“基本需求”:良好的收入和愉快的工作条件。
content.en_text: Maslow identified five important needs which he placed in a triangle : at the bottom of the triangle he put what he called “ basic needs”: good pay and pleasant working conditions .

content.cn_text: 然后，在基本需求之上，他提出了“安全需求”——安全的工作条件和工作保障。
content.en_text: Then, above the basic needs, he put “security needs”-safe working conditions and job security.

content.cn_text: 他称之为“社交”的第三种需求——在工作中有朋友，与老板相处融洽。
content.en_text: His third need he called “social”-the need to have friends at work and get on with the boss.

content.cn_text: 第四个方面是“地位需求”——职位和社会认可。
content.en_text: Fourth werft ” status needs”-a job title and social recognition .

content.cn_text: 最后，在三角的顶端，马斯洛确定了晋升的需要——拥有一份具有挑战性的工作，并有晋升的机会。
content.en_text: Finally, at the top of the triangle , Maslow identified the need for advancement -to have a challenging job with opportunities for promotion .

content.cn_text: 他的理论并不完美，但它是一个方便的框架，可以用来观察人们对工作的不同需求和期望，而且，更重要的是，我相信它今天仍然有效。
content.en_text: His theory isn't perfect, but it's a convenient framework for viewing the different needs and expectations of work that people have, and, what's more, I believe it s still valid today.

content.cn_text: 在21世纪初对全职员工进行的一项调查中，当被问及是什么给了他们工作满意度时，72%的人说有一个鼓舞人心的领导。
content.en_text: During an early twenty-first century survey of full-time employees , when asked what gave them job satisfaction , 72% said having an inspirational leader.

content.cn_text: 55%的受访者对工作的挑战性感到满意，50%的人对高薪感到满意，40%的人认为工作时间灵活。
content.en_text: 55% of those questioned found satisfaction in the challenging natureof their work, 50% by being paid well, and 40% said flexible working hour.

content.cn_text: 因此，我们可以看到，这表明今天，员工对工作场所领导素质的看法是影响公司作为一个好工作场所的重要因素。
content.en_text: Consequently, we can see that this indicates that today, an employee's opinion of the quality of the leadership in their workplace is an important factor influencing belief in the company as a good place towork.

content.cn_text: 动机因一个人的工作生活和居住地而异。研究表明，不同国家的人有不同的目标。
content.en_text: Motivation varies over a person's working life and also according to where they live.Studies show that people have different goals in different countries.

content.cn_text: 例如，良好的生活方式在西班牙似乎比在瑞典更重要。
content.en_text: For example, a good lifestyle would appear to be more important in Spain than it is in Sweden.

content.cn_text: 金融安全在美国比在英国更重要。
content.en_text: Financial security is more important in the US than in the UK.

content.cn_text: 工作中的社交对德国和澳大利亚的员工来说都很重要，但在英国和意大利却不是最重要的。
content.en_text: Social contact at work is important to workers in both Germany and Australia, but not of top importance in Britain and Italy.

content.cn_text: 至于高地位——这在美国比在欧洲更重要。
content.en_text: As to high status -this is more important in the USA than to Europeans.

content.cn_text: 这些结果表明了国家之间的明显差异，但我认为不应该太认真。
content.en_text: These results indicateclear differences between countries, but I don't think they should be taken too seriously .

content.cn_text: 有许多理论试图解释动机的本质。这些理论在一定程度上是正确的，总的来说，它们都有助于解释某些人在某些时候的行为。
content.en_text: There have been many theories which have attempted to explain the nature of motivation.These theories are partially true, and by and large all help to explain the behaviour of certain people at certain times.

content.cn_text: 然而，没有一个能真正提供所有的答案。
content.en_text: However, none really provides all the answers.

content.cn_text: 公司能做的最好的事情就是为员工提供一个自我激励的环境。
content.en_text: The best a company can do is to provide people with the right environment to be self-motivated .

content.cn_text: 如果他们是自我激励的，他们会表现得很好
content.en_text: If they are self-motivated, they will perform well

groups.desc:
Questions 31-32
Complete the diagram below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS and/or A NUMBER for each answer.

items.analysis: 文中明确提到“needs and expectations will affect each person's behaviour”，这与题干中的“influence/affect people's”相对应。因此，答案是“behaviour”。当定位词“affect”出现时，考生应注意其后紧随的内容，这里答案即为“behaviour”。
因为题干中提到“achievement of goals”，而原文中提到“the achievement of these goals will, in turn, lead to job satisfaction”，很明显原文指出达成目标将带来工作满意度。依据这个因果关系，我们得出正确答案是 job satisfaction。

groups.desc:
Questions 33-35
Complete the sentences below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS and/or A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect:
Taylor wrote his theory in the $17837$ century.
Taylor's theory was that people only worked for $17838$
Later research concluded that some people preferred to work $17839$

items.analysis: 这道题的关键在于准确识别时间信息。文中提到"In the 19th century"，这直接对应了题目要求的世纪。Taylor是一个重要的定位词，帮助我们在原文中找到相关信息。注意，19th可以写作数字19，也可以写作单词nineteenth。这里考察了你对世纪表达的理解和灵活运用能力。另外，要注意区分19世纪和之后提到的20世纪，避免混淆。记住，在听力中，时间信息往往是重要的答案线索，尤其是在涉及历史事件或理论发展的题目中。

这道题的关键在于准确理解和定位Taylor的理论。题干中提到"Taylor's theory"，这是一个明确的定位词，引导我们在听力材料中寻找与Frederick Taylor相关的内容。在原文中，我们可以听到"Frederick Taylor put forward the theory"这个信号词，表明接下来将介绍Taylor的理论内容。原文明确指出Taylor认为"workers were only interested in making money"，这直接对应了题目所问的内容。答案"making money"完全符合题目要求的不超过三个词的限制。要注意的是，不要被后文提到的"financial reward"所迷惑，因为那是在讨论20世纪早期的研究，而不是Taylor的理论。做这道题时，重点是要准确捕捉与Taylor相关的信息，并迅速提取出他的理论核心。

这道题的关键在于理解"Later research"与"An early study"的对应关系。文中提到了一项早期研究，这与题干中的"Later research"形成了时间上的对比。研究结果显示，人们在团队中工作时更快乐、更有效率，这就暗示了人们更喜欢在团队中工作。题目要求填写人们更喜欢如何工作，而不是在哪里工作，所以正确答案是"in teams"而不是"in coal mining company"。这道题考察了你对细节信息的捕捉能力，以及对比较信息的理解能力。记住，在听力中，要特别注意与研究、工作方式相关的词汇，这些往往是答案的关键所在。

groups.desc:
Questions 36-37
Complete the diagram below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS and/or A NUMBER for each answer.

items.analysis: 题干中的定位词“36”和“needs”帮助我们在原文中找到了相关信息。信号词“bottom”（底部）指示了位置，表明答案位于三角形的底部。原文中明确提到“at the bottom of the triangle he put what he called 'basic needs'”，即“在三角形的底部，他放置了他称之为‘基本需求’的内容。因此，答案是‘basic’”。
文中提到马斯洛的需求层次理论，并按照顺序介绍了各个层次的需求。第四层明确提到的是“status needs”，即地位需求。因此，答案是“status”。

groups.desc:
Questions 38-39
Complete the table below.
Look at the table and notice that in this section you need to listen for percentages.

items.analysis: 这道题的关键在于准确识别和匹配信息。文中提到了一项关于工作满意度的调查，其中列举了几个不同的满意度来源及其对应的百分比。"Type of work"在原文中对应的是"challenging nature of their work"，这是一个很好的同义替换。55%这个数字正好与这个描述相对应，因此是正确答案。要注意的是，文中提到的其他百分比（如72%和50%）分别对应其他的满意度来源，不要混淆。做这类题目时，要特别留意百分比数字及其相关描述，快速在脑中建立对应关系。同时，也要注意题目要求填写的具体内容，本题要求填写百分比，因此答案应该是一个百分比数字。

同学，这道题的答案是40%，原因如下：首先，题目要求我们填写"灵活工作时间"（flexible working hours）对应的百分比。在原文中，我们可以清楚地听到"40% said flexible working hours"，这直接对应了题目要求。其次，这个句子是在列举一系列关于工作满意度因素的百分比，包括领导、工作性质、薪资等，而"灵活工作时间"是最后一个提到的因素，对应的正是40%。因此，40%是唯一正确的答案。在做这类题目时，要特别注意听数字和与之相关的描述，并与题目要求的信息进行匹配。

groups.desc:
Question 40
Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

items.title: What does the speaker say about workers in the UK?

options.title: A
options.content: They want to have a good lifestyle.

options.title: B
options.content: Socialising at work is not a priority for them.

options.title: C
options.content: They want to earn a lot of money.

options.title: D
options.content: Having a job title is a priority for them.

items.analysis: 文中明确指出社交在工作中对英国工人来说"not of top importance"（不是最重要的），这与选项B"Socialising at work is not a priority for them"（在工作中社交对他们来说不是优先事项）的含义一致。这表明英国工人虽然可能重视工作中的社交，但并不将其视为最优先的事项。
